page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.8xlarge is an x86_64 architecture general-purpose server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs utilizing an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 10 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place the ecs.g7se.8xlarge in the average performance tier for most compute and memory operations. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a single-core stress-ng score of 1856.69 and a multi-core score of 31060.72, supported by a passmark CPU score of 36697.24. Memory performance is a notable strength, with the passmark memory score ranking in the top 25% at 2777.67, alongside a read-only small block memory bandwidth of 1682.59 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves 31.34 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and demonstrates strong performance in small-model LLM text generation, reaching 301.47 tokens per second for a 135M parameter model.

From a resource density perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without the virtualization overhead or resource contention found in shared-core environments. However, the lack of bundled local storage and a moderate 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth represent key architectural tradeoffs that require external storage provisioning. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring balanced compute and memory resources without the premium cost of GPU or high-capacity local NVMe drives. It is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.9 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor, it provides 128.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data indicates average CPU performance, with a passmark CPU score of 36697.24, but strong memory performance, highlighted by a passmark memory score in the top 25%. It delivers 31.34 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 301.47 tokens per second in small-model LLM text generation. This instance is suited for balanced workloads such as database operations, web serving, and software compilation.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.8xlarge is a general-purpose server with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 10 Gbps network baseline, delivering balanced compute and strong memory performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7se.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and dedicated CPU allocation for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 server with dedicated Intel Xeon processors and strong memory performance for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B processor with 32 physical cores and vCPUs
Balanced memory configuration of 128.0 GB RAM providing 4.0 GB per core
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware, relying on network storage
Best suited for database operations, static web serving, and software compilation
